overview: Neobrand is a five-color Modern deck that leverages cheap creatures and untap/haste synergies to generate explosive early turns. By chaining Summoner's Pact and Allosaurus Rider, players can bypass normal turn structure to attack with tapped creatures like Griselbrand immediately. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents through unexpected damage spikes and relentless card advantage.
win_conditions: Griselbrand attacking immediately after being untapped and given haste
Swinging for lethal damage using multiple Pact and Rider untap chains in a single turn
Outdrawing opponents through repeated Summoner's Pact resolution while maintaining board presence

keep_criteria: Hands containing Summoner's Pact paired with at least one creature
Early mana sources to support five-color requirements
Allosaurus Rider or Griselbrand with a way to untap them
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or card draw
Heavy lands with no creatures or Pact
Griselbrand without an untap or haste source

early_game: Play cheap creatures and cast Summoner's Pact to draw cards and discard
Use Allosaurus Rider to untap creatures and grant haste for immediate attacks
Prioritize mana development to enable five-color casting by turn three
mid_game: Chain Pact and Rider to generate multiple attacks per turn
Stabilize against removal by keeping Pact in hand to replace lost creatures
Begin setting up Griselbrand as a primary threat once mana is established
late_game: Attack with Griselbrand repeatedly using untap loops
Maintain card advantage by cycling through Pact draws
Close out games with overwhelming board presence or direct damage from haste creatures

key_cards.name: Griselbrand
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and late-game threat
key_cards.notes: Enters tapped but can attack immediately if untapped and given haste by Rider or Pact; prioritize protecting it once it resolves

key_cards.name: Summoner's Pact
key_cards.notes: Use the second mode to untap a creature and exile a card from your graveyard; chain with Rider for multiple attacks per turn

key_cards.name: Allosaurus Rider
key_cards.notes: Activates to untap a creature and give it +1/+1 and haste; sequence before attacking to maximize damage output

sequencing_tips: Always untap and grant haste before declaring attackers to avoid wasting tempo
Chain Pact's untap mode after Rider to attack the same creature twice in one turn
Manage your graveyard carefully since Pact can exile cards you might need for other effects
Hold up Pact in hand to respond to removal rather than committing it too early

matchups.advice: Use Pact to draw into answers and swing early with haste creatures before they stabilize
Go wide with cheap creatures and use Pact to outvalue them; protect Griselbrand with counters or evasion if possible
Apply early pressure with Rider and Pact chains to force them to play defensively
